It’s not just misleading headlines or exaggerated claims—it’s the kind of thing that can make people feel anxious or even misled about real-world issues. I come from a background where practicality and truth mattered—my father was a mechanic, and we learned that if something doesn’t work in real life, it’s not true. That same sense of honesty applies to the digital world. I try to manage fake content by being more selective about the sources I follow. I don’t trust anything that sounds too good to be true, especially when it comes to health or financial advice. I also pause before sharing something that makes me uneasy. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend several hours daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: Fake content on social media is a growing problem, and as someone who spends hours every day scrolling through feeds, I’ve seen it firsthand. It’s not just misleading headlines or exaggerated claims—it’s the kind of thing that can make people feel anxious or even misled about real-world issues. I come from a background where practicality and truth mattered—my father was a mechanic, and we learned that if something doesn’t work in real life, it’s not true. That same sense of honesty applies to the digital world. I try to manage fake content by being more selective about the sources I follow. I don’t trust anything that sounds too good to be true, especially when it comes to health or financial advice. I also pause before sharing something that makes me uneasy. My curiosity drives me to look deeper—sometimes I’ll check the original post or see if it’s been reported by a reliable news outlet. I’ve learned that just because something is viral doesn’t mean it’s true. In my daily life, I’m careful about what I believe and how I speak to others, especially my family. I think that if we all take a moment to question what we see online, we can protect ourselves and others from misinformation. It’s not about rejecting technology, but about using it with wisdom and caution.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The persona is a curious, moderately neurotic, and highly extraverted man from Bari who spends significant time on social media. His background in practical trades and family values informs his skepticism toward exaggerated claims. The opinion draws on real-life experience, personal values, and a need for truth in everyday decisions. The tone is grounded, practical, and reflective, consistent with a working-class Italian man who values honesty and community.
response_parsed.tone: practical, grounded, reflective, slightly skeptical but not dismissive
citations: Italian Ministry of Health, 2023 - Digital Literacy Report
